chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P No.D-3785 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on office objection. For order on MA No.16602 / 2017. For katcha peshi. 07-02-2018 Mr. Ali Asghar Mangi advocate for petitioners along with petitioners. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto A.A.G. Sindh along with I.O / SIP Manzoor Hussain PS Kot Laloo. Comments on behalf of respondents No.3 and 4 filed by learned A.A.G. are taken on record. Petitioners are present along with their counsel. Petitioner No.1 submits that she being sui juris has solemnized marriage with petitioner No.2. Learned counsel for the petitioners submits that such act of the petitioner No.1 has antagonized her parents who have lodged a false FIR bearing crime No.65 / 2017 at PS Kotlaloo, District Khairpur Miras regarding her abduction against the petitioner No.2 and his relatives. Petitioners submit that they are living together as husband and wife but local police at the instance of private respondents is harassing them without any justification. Notice sent to private respondents but they are absent without any intimation. Since the petitioners have married with each other and living together as husband and wife, therefore, under the circumstances, official respondents are directed to make sure that there should not be caused harassment to the petitioners in violation of law. In the meantime, Investigating Officer of the case present in Court is directed to record statement of petitioner No.1 Mst.Reema in the office learned A.A.G. and file one copy of statement before the learned trial Court for passing appropriate order.

Since the petitioners have married with each other and living together as husband and wife, therefore, under the circumstances, official respondents are directed to make sure that there should not be caused harassment to the petitioners in violation of law. In the meantime, Investigating Officer of the case present in Court is directed to record statement of petitioner No.1 Mst.Reema in the office learned A.A.G. and file one copy of statement before the learned trial Court for passing appropriate order. Till then, it is ordered that no arrest shall be made in the said FIR. In view of the above, this petition stands disposed of along with listed application. JUDGE JUDGE *Abdullah Channa/PS*
